Description: In my previous games, which were primarily story-driven simulation games, I required a robust, flexible, and scalable dialogue system to handle cross-actor interactions effectively. To meet these requirements, I developed a custom dialogue system from scratch. This system is action and condition based, allowing it to execute specific actions and evaluate game conditions seamlessly. Additionally, it includes variable functionality to reuse values across different dialogues, as well as checkpoints to enable branching dialogues based on specific criteria.
Features: A user-friendly dialogue system designed for seamless integration by designers.
Modular architecture to facilitate the addition of new features with ease.
Simplified implementation process for efficient deployment and maintenance.
Title: Dialogue System

Texts: The system is designed with a modular architecture, utilizing C# reflection to simplify the creation of new actions, conditions, or features. By inheriting from predefined types and applying attributes, new features can be seamlessly integrated into the system without additional configuration. Dialogue, conditions, and actions are managed and stored using a ScriptableObject architecture, ensuring efficient organization and scalability.

After developing a robust, flexible, and scalable dialogue system from scratch, I recognized the need for a designer-friendly interface to manage and create all the dialogues efficiently. After researching various options, I decided to leverage Unity's new UI Toolkit and GraphView systems to build a custom graph-based editor for managing dialogue nodes.

I was pleasantly surprised by how straightforward it was to create a node-based editor from scratch using these tools. The resulting dialogue editor exceeded expectations, enabling my team to seamlessly create and manage dialogues. Its intuitive node-based design made it easy for the team to understand and utilize all the features effectively, significantly improving workflow and productivity.
Contents.Title: Graph & Node Based Editor Tool
The Result
Texts: I successfully implemented this system in several of my previous projects, including Legendary Fish Hunter, which was released in 2024. I was highly satisfied with the results, as it allowed my team to efficiently create well-structured and engaging dialogues without spending excessive time on connecting elements manually. The graph and node-based system proved to be an ideal solution for streamlining dialogue creation and enhancing the overall workflow.

This tool was specifically developed to address the unique needs of my previous company. However, with their permission, I am sharing its core features here solely to showcase my recent work. Please note that this page is intended purely as a portfolio to demonstrate my expertise and is not intended for marketing or public use, as the tool remains proprietary to the company.

Description: In addition to a robust, flexible, and scalable dialogue system, my previous games also required a powerful quest system to manage and drive the game's storyline. Finding a ready-made solution that met the game's specific needs without introducing unnecessary features and performance overhead proved challenging. As a result, I decided to develop a custom quest system from scratch to fulfill these requirements.
Features: Seamless integration with custom dialogue system for dynamic Player-NPC interactions.
Supports in-game cutscenes with Unity’s Timeline system and dynamic triggers.
Custom quest-tree editor for visualizing and managing quest flow and dependencies.
Reduces bug-fixing time by 40%, improving overall team efficiency.
Title: Quest System

Contents.Text: The system integrates seamlessly with the custom dialogue system I built, allowing for dynamic interactions between the player and NPCs that contribute to the active storyline. It also supports in-game cutscenes using Unity's Timeline system, enhanced with dynamic triggers. These triggers enable quests to be initiated through various means, such as NPC interactions, entering specific areas, or interacting with objects or buttons. This flexibility ensures that the quest system is tailored to the game's needs while remaining efficient and intuitive for the team to use.

Contents.Title: Quest Tree Editor
Texts: After developing a custom quest system from scratch, I encountered challenges in managing a large volume of story content, especially during the post-production stage. My team and I often faced situations where players would get stuck in certain quests without a clear understanding of what went wrong. To address this issue, I created a custom quest-tree editor that provides a comprehensive view of all quests in the project and their flow from start to finish.

Upon using the editor for the first time, I realized its immense potential. Not only did it resolve the post-production challenges by making it easier to identify and fix issues, but it also allowed us to check for overflowing references in quests, preventing errors before the game build. Additionally, by leveraging Unity's SceneManagement API, the editor performs scene checks and generates snapshots of quest usage. This enables the team to quickly identify which scenes utilize specific quests and which NPCs contribute to them.

The introduction of this tool significantly improved our workflow. Based on the team’s experience, we reduced the time spent fixing in-game quest bugs by 40%, freeing up resources to create even more quests and enrich the game’s content.

This tool was specifically developed to address the unique needs of my previous company. However, with their permission, I am sharing its core features here solely to showcase my recent work. Please note that this page is intended purely as a portfolio to demonstrate my expertise and is not intended for marketing or public use, as the tool remains proprietary to the company.
Contents.Title: The Result

Description: The motivation for creating Safe Event arose from recurring post-production bugs reported by users. These bugs often revolved around specific game features not functioning correctly, or worse, failing entirely. Upon investigation, most of these issues were traced back to unhandled exceptions in methods invoked by certain events.
Title: Safe Event
Features: .InvokeSafely() ensures uninterrupted listener execution despite exceptions.
Supports  up to 10 generic arguments for flexible event handling.
Collapsible inspector view reduces clutter and improves debugging.
Enables safe invocation of System.Action and System.Func

Texts: The root cause of the problem was the default behavior of event invocation, if a listener method in an event throws an exception, the event execution halts, preventing subsequent listeners from being called. Unfortunately, this behavior cannot be bypassed without ensuring proper exception handling within each listener.

While handling exceptions is a fundamental programming practice, fast-paced development often prioritizes core features to accelerate testing. This creates a need for a utility that ensures uninterrupted event invocation even when some listeners encounter unhandled exceptions. To address this, I developed Safe Event, a robust utility that guarantees safe event execution without compromising flexibility or performance.

Contents.Text: Safe Event ensures that all registered actions or persistent actions within an event are invoked without interruption, even when one or more listeners encounter unhandled exceptions. This guarantees that no listener prevents others from being executed. With Safe Event, you can confidently add as many listeners as needed without worrying about runtime errors causing unintended behavior. This feature is invaluable for complex systems where multiple components rely on the same event triggers, ensuring stability and reliability across all listeners.
Contents.Title: Safe Invocation
Extended Functionality
Texts: Safe Event works similarly to UnityEvent but introduces several key enhancements that expand its usability. One of the standout features is its compatibility with displaying multiple arguments directly in the Unity inspector. Unlike UnityEvent, which limits argument handling, Safe Event supports up to 10 generic arguments, making it ideal for events requiring complex data structures or multiple inputs. This capability eliminates the need for workarounds and simplifies event management for intricate scenarios.

Additionally, Safe Event’s flexibility ensures a seamless transition for developers familiar with UnityEvent, while providing enhanced functionality to meet more advanced requirements. These improvements make Safe Event an indispensable tool for projects demanding robust and dynamic event handling.

Contents.Title: Collapsible On Inspector
Texts: Safe Event enhances the inspector experience with collapsible listener views, which improve organization and focus. In traditional UnityEvent setups, all listeners are displayed at once, leading to visual clutter when dealing with numerous events. Safe Event solves this by allowing developers to collapse or expand individual listeners directly in the inspector.

This feature is particularly useful in large projects where managing events can become cumbersome. By collapsing unused listeners, developers can focus on the specific aspects of the event that matter at the moment, significantly improving the workflow and reducing distractions. The collapsible views also make debugging easier by presenting a cleaner and more streamlined interface.

Contents.Title: Extensions for System.Action and System.Func
Texts: Safe Event extends System.Action and System.Func with safe invocation capabilities. Even if exceptions occur in a listener, they are handled gracefully, ensuring uninterrupted execution. Developers can still catch exceptions on the listener side if needed, maintaining flexibility in error management. This extension makes it easier to integrate Safe Event into existing codebases, offering a seamless transition from traditional event handling to a more robust and reliable system.

Title: Easing Library
Description: Creating smooth and visually appealing animations is crucial for enhancing the overall quality of a game. However, manually searching for easing formulas and implementing them into each animation can be time-consuming and inefficient. To address this challenge, I developed my own easing library, enabling me to easily reuse a wide range of pre-built easing functions across multiple animations. This approach significantly streamlines the development process, allowing for faster implementation and more consistent, high-quality animations throughout the game.
Features: Provides various easing functions for smooth and dynamic animations.
Uses normalized time for easy integration across different animation durations.
Ensures smooth, natural transitions for more polished and fluid animations.
Flexible evaluation for precise control over animation progress at any point.

Texts: The Easing Libary provides a set of mathematical formulas, known as easing functions, that help control the rate of change of values over time. These formulas are essential for creating smooth animations, transitions, and effects in various programming environments, particularly in web development, game development, and user interface (UI) design. By using easing functions, developers can make animations feel more natural, engaging, and responsive by simulating how objects accelerate and decelerate.

This Library features a comprehensive collection of easing functions, including linear, quadratic, cubic, quartic, quintic, exponential, circular, and more. These functions offer smooth transitions and animations, with varying degrees of acceleration and deceleration. It also includes unique pulse-based easing functions like BellCurvePulse, RoundedPeakPulse, and SharpPeakPulse.

We can choose from a variety of easing functions to match the specific motion characteristics required for their animations. Whether it’s a smooth acceleration or a dramatic overshoot, the Ease class allows for precise control over how a value changes over time, resulting in highly customizable and visually appealing animations.
Contents.Title: Wide Range of Easing Functions

Texts: This Library features a comprehensive collection of easing functions, including linear, quadratic, cubic, quartic, quintic, exponential, circular, and more. These functions offer smooth transitions and animations, with varying degrees of acceleration and deceleration. It also includes unique pulse-based easing functions like BellCurvePulse, RoundedPeakPulse, and SharpPeakPulse.

We can choose from a variety of easing functions to match the specific motion characteristics required for their animations. Whether it’s a smooth acceleration or a dramatic overshoot, the Ease class allows for precise control over how a value changes over time, resulting in highly customizable and visually appealing animations.

The Easing library is designed for easy integration into your animations. By passing a normalized value (usually between 0 and 1) to the appropriate easing function, you can achieve smooth transitions and dynamic effects. The Evaluate method can be used to evaluate any easing function at a specific point in time, allowing for precise control over your animations.

The Easing Library is a perfect tool for developers looking to enhance their animations and transitions. By incorporating easing functions into your projects, you can create more lifelike and engaging experiences. Whether you need simple linear motions or complex, natural animations, the Ease class provides the flexibility and functionality required to achieve your desired effects.  For more advanced control, experiment with combining different easing functions or modifying the normalized time for unique animation outcomes.
Contents.Title: Easy To Implement

Title: Data Manager
Features: Automatically loads and saves data with a simple .Save() method.
Supports PlayerPrefs, Google Play Games, Firebase, and custom providers.
Saves data across multiple providers at once for added reliability.
Easily extendable to accommodate new data types or additional features.
User-friendly interface to manage data directly within the Unity Editor.
Add functionality to data classes with attributes, like Unity’s ContextMenu.
Allows for rapid iteration by tweaking data in play mode during testing.
Description: As a game developer, one of the most crucial yet challenging aspects of game development is managing game data. Whether it's player progress, settings, or any other form of in-game information, the need for a robust and flexible data management system is undeniable. However, after evaluating numerous off-the-shelf data management solutions, I realized that most of them came with unnecessary complexity and excessive features that often added more bloat than value to my project.

Contents.Title: Simple Data Management
Texts: At its core, this system is built for simplicity. By inheriting a data class from a predefined base class, data is automatically loaded during game initialization. Saving data is equally easy, simply call the .Save() method on the data class, and the system will handle everything for you.

This approach eliminates the need for complicated setup or configuration. Whether you're managing player stats, settings, or any other type of game data, the tool makes the process straightforward and seamless.

Contents.Title: Multiple Data Provider Support
Contents.Text: Flexibility is key in any game development project, and this tool excels in this area by supporting multiple data providers, including PlayerPrefs, Google Play Games, Firebase, or even your own custom provider. The system also allows for simultaneous data saving across multiple providers, ensuring that your player’s data is securely stored in more than one place for added reliability. With support for these diverse providers, you can easily tailor your data management solution to meet the specific needs of your game, ensuring that your data is always safe, accessible, and backed up across platforms.

Texts: This tool is designed with ease of integration in mind. From loading to saving data, everything is handled automatically, so you don’t have to worry about manual processes. This makes the tool highly effective for fast-paced development cycles.

While this tool is simple to use, it also offers a high degree of customization. Whether you want to extend existing functionality or add new features, the tool is flexible enough to fit your game's specific requirements. Customize how data is loaded, saved, or distributed across providers without worrying about restrictions or limitations.

Contents.Title: Introducing the Custom DataManager Editor for Unity
Texts: Alongside the data management system, I developed a custom DataManager Editor for Unity to simplify the process of editing and manipulating data directly within the Unity Editor. This addition allows you to manage your game’s data without needing to write any code, significantly improving the workflow for both individual developers and teams.

The DataManager Editor comes with a user-friendly interface that makes it incredibly easy to manage and modify data within the Unity Editor. No more navigating through complex menus or manually updating data files; everything can be done directly in the editor, streamlining the entire data management process. This tool ensures that your team can quickly tweak data, adjust values, and make changes during development without needing to interrupt the flow of the game.

Contents.Title: Modular Functionality with C# Reflections
Texts: To enhance flexibility, the DataManager Editor supports modular functionality using C# reflections. By simply applying attributes to methods, similar to Unity’s ContextMenu attribute, you can easily add new actions to your data classes. This makes it possible to perform dynamic actions on specific data without additional coding or complex setup, further simplifying the process of managing game data in Unity.

The DataManager Editor also supports in-editor testing, allowing your team to tweak data directly in play mode. This feature enables rapid iteration and debugging, so new features and changes can be tested immediately without needing to exit the editor or write additional code. It streamlines the testing process, allowing your team to quickly identify and resolve issues without interrupting development.

This custom Data Management System and DataManager Editor for Unity offers a streamlined, powerful solution for handling game data with minimal effort. With features that support multiple data providers, modular functionality, in-editor testing, and a user-friendly interface, it enhances both the maintainability of your game’s data and the efficiency of your development process. Whether you’re working solo or as part of a team, this tool will help you manage data effortlessly, so you can focus on creating the best possible game experience for your players.

Description: UI Highlighter is designed to help players, especially younger audiences, navigate games more intuitively and without confusion. This tool provides a simple yet effective way to highlight key areas of the game, such as buttons, images, or other important interface elements, guiding players through tutorials in a visually engaging way. The tool is optimized for use in children’s games where clarity and ease of use are paramount, ensuring that instructions are accessible even without text.
Features: Easily highlight key elements with a simple inverse mask effect to focus player attention.
Built-in animations with smooth tweening and easing effects for an engaging, fluid experience.
Customizable highlight shapes allow for precise and flexible visual cues for various elements
User-friendly editor lets designers set up highlights without coding, streamlining the process.
Title: UI Highlighter

Texts: To make the tutorial experience more engaging and fluid, the UI Highlighter includes built-in tweening and easing effects. These animations transition highlights smoothly, making the tutorial not just informative but visually dynamic and fun. The smoothness of these animations ensures that players remain immersed in the gameplay, while the visual cues are soft yet effective enough to maintain their focus. This feature helps create an enjoyable, seamless experience that integrates naturally into the game’s flow, making the tutorial process less jarring.
Contents.Title: Engaging Animations with Smooth Tweening & Easing Effects
Customizable Highlight Shapes for Flexibility
Texts: Every tutorial scenario is different, which is why the In-Game Tutorial Highlighter provides flexible options for highlight shapes. Whether you’re drawing attention to a button, a menu, or a game object, you can select from various shapes that best fit the element being highlighted. This customization ensures that the tutorial remains clear and precise, allowing for the necessary adjustments to suit different game layouts, screen sizes, or design requirements. No matter the context, this flexibility allows you to tailor the experience to best match the needs of your game.

Setting up the UI Highlighter is a breeze. The system allows you to highlight specific elements within the game, such as buttons or images, by using an inverse mask effect to darken the surrounding screen. This draws attention directly to the areas you want players to focus on, creating a simple and clear visual cue. With no need for complex coding or extensive setup, you can quickly begin enhancing the tutorial experience and get straight into testing.

UI Highlighter is the perfect solution for developers creating games targeted at young players or those new to gaming. By focusing on simplicity and accessibility, it ensures that players of all ages can quickly grasp how to interact with the game without being distracted or overwhelmed by text-heavy instructions. Whether you’re developing a first-time gaming experience or enhancing an existing one, this tool provides an easy and effective way to integrate visually engaging tutorials directly into your game, creating a seamless, user-friendly learning process that keeps players focused, entertained, and on track.
